name: AT&T Inc.
description: Second largest US wireless carrier. Major spectrum holder across all bands. Largest AWS-3 bidder at $18.2B. FirstNet nationwide public safety network operator.
govt_contracts: FirstNet nationwide public safety broadband network ($46.5B contract), major DoD and federal agency contracts
defense_intel_connections: FirstNet operator (Band 14). Historical AT&T involvement in NSA surveillance programs (Room 641A). Major government communications provider. AT&T Government Solutions division.
subsidiaries: ["AT&T Mobility Spectrum LLC", "AT&T Mobility LLC", "FiberTower Spectrum Holdings", "Cricket Wireless", "FirstNet Built with AT&T"]
notes: Acquired FiberTower (2017) for mmWave spectrum. Operates FirstNet for public safety. Divested WarnerMedia to Discovery (2022).

name: Alaska Native Wireless
description: Designated entity that won massive PCS spectrum in Auction 35. Controversial use of DE program.
notes: Won $2.9B in PCS C/F Block spectrum in Auction 35 as designated entity. Controversial -- alleged to be front for larger companies seeking DE discounts. Early example of DE program abuse later seen with Dish/Northstar/SNR.

name: Anterix Inc.
description: Formerly pdvWireless. Holds 900 MHz spectrum nationwide. Leases spectrum to utilities for private LTE/5G networks.
notes: Unique business model: only nationwide holder of 900 MHz broadband spectrum. Leases to electric utilities (Ameren, Evergy, Duke Energy) for grid modernization. Also bid in CBRS auction.

name: Barat Wireless LP
description: Entity that won AWS-1 spectrum in Auction 66.
notes: Won $535M in AWS-1 spectrum (Auction 66).

name: Bluewater Wireless LP
description: Investment entity that won 600 MHz spectrum in the incentive auction.
notes: Won $131M in 600 MHz spectrum (Auction 1000). Limited public information on ownership.

name: Boeing Company
description: Aerospace and defense company. Builds military communications satellites and aircraft with spectrum-dependent systems.
govt_contracts: WGS (Wideband Global SATCOM), P-8A Poseidon, KC-46, satellite communications systems
defense_intel_connections: Builds Wideband Global SATCOM (WGS) military satellites operating in X and Ka bands. Military aircraft communications systems. Boeing Satellite Systems.
notes: WGS satellites provide critical DoD communications in X-band and Ka-band. Stakeholder in military spectrum policy.

name: Cavalier Wireless
description: Entity that won 700 MHz spectrum in Auction 73.
notes: Won $214M in 700 MHz spectrum in Auction 73.

name: CenturyTel Broadband
description: CenturyTel (now Lumen Technologies) entity that won 700 MHz spectrum.
defense_intel_connections: Lumen (formerly CenturyLink/Level 3) operates significant government fiber backbone. DISA contracts.
notes: CenturyTel became CenturyLink (2009) then Lumen Technologies (2020). Won $79M in 700 MHz spectrum.

name: Channel 51 License Co LLC
description: Spectrum holding company that bid in multiple auctions including AWS-3 and 600 MHz.
notes: Won $437M in AWS-3 (Auction 97) and $285M in 600 MHz incentive auction. Named for TV Channel 51 (698-704 MHz).

name: Charter Communications
description: Second largest US cable company (Spectrum brand). Entered wireless via Spectrum Mobile MVNO. CBRS spectrum holder.
notes: Spent ~$464M on CBRS spectrum in Auction 105. MVNO on Verizon network.

name: Clearwire Corporation
description: Held massive 2.5 GHz spectrum portfolio. Backed by Sprint, Intel, Comcast, Google, TWC. WiMAX then LTE. Acquired by Sprint 2013.
notes: At peak held more 2.5 GHz spectrum than any other company. Backed by Sprint, Intel, Comcast, Google, TWC, Bright House ($3.2B investment 2008). Sprint acquired 2013. Spectrum now held by T-Mobile.

name: Columbia Capital
description: Private equity firm focused on communications. Active spectrum investor through multiple entities.
notes: Alexandria, VA based PE firm. Bid via Radiate Holdings and NewLevel II in C-band auction. Also active in 3.45 GHz auction.

name: Comcast Corporation
description: Largest US cable company. Entered wireless via Xfinity Mobile (MVNO on Verizon). Acquired spectrum in 600 MHz and CBRS auctions.
defense_intel_connections: NBCUniversal parent. Major content/distribution company with government relations.
notes: Was part of SpectrumCo (AWS-1 auction) which sold spectrum to Verizon for $3.6B. Spent $1.7B on 600 MHz spectrum.

name: Cox Communications
description: Third largest US cable company (privately held). CBRS spectrum holder. Was part of SpectrumCo consortium.
notes: Was part of SpectrumCo (AWS-1 auction) with Comcast, TWC, Bright House. Bid ~$316M in 700 MHz auction.

name: Cricket Communications
description: Prepaid wireless carrier. Formerly Leap Wireless. Acquired by AT&T in 2014.
notes: Originally Leap Wireless International. Won $470M in AWS-1 spectrum (Auction 66). AT&T acquired for $1.19B in 2014. Now AT&T prepaid brand.

name: Deutsche Telekom AG
description: German telecom giant. Majority owner of T-Mobile US. Controls the third-largest US spectrum portfolio through T-Mobile.
defense_intel_connections: German government owns ~32% stake via KfW. Foreign ownership of US spectrum subject to CFIUS review. T-Mobile US operates independently.
notes: Holds ~48.4% economic ownership and ~52% voting power in T-Mobile US. German government is largest shareholder via KfW development bank. SoftBank (now fully divested) was second largest T-Mobile shareholder.

name: Dish Network Corporation
description: Massive spectrum hoarder turned carrier. Spent over $25B on spectrum across multiple auctions through shell companies. Building Open RAN 5G network under DoJ mandate.
govt_contracts: DoJ/FCC buildout requirement as condition of T-Mobile/Sprint merger approval
defense_intel_connections: Spectrum holdings overlap with DoD interest bands. Charlie Ergen strategy of accumulating spectrum led to FCC scrutiny over designated entity abuse.
subsidiaries: ["Northstar Wireless LLC", "SNR Wireless LicenseCo LLC", "ParkerB.com Wireless LLC", "Weminuche LLC", "Frontier Wireless LLC", "DISH Wireless LLC", "Boost Mobile"]
notes: Used shell companies (Northstar, SNR) to claim designated entity discounts in AWS-3 auction -- lost $3.3B in credits when FCC found them Dish-controlled. Merged with EchoStar (2024). Must build nationwide 5G or lose spectrum.
source: FCC filings, SEC 10-K, court records, press reports

name: EchoStar Corporation
description: Satellite communications and technology company. Merged with Dish Network in 2024. Parent of combined Dish/EchoStar spectrum portfolio.
notes: Charlie Ergen controls both EchoStar and Dish. Merged in 2024. Combined entity holds massive spectrum portfolio across multiple bands. Hughes provides satellite broadband.

name: Evergy Kansas Central
description: Electric utility that won 2.5 GHz spectrum for grid communications.
notes: Won $13M in 2.5 GHz spectrum (Auction 108). Utility acquiring spectrum for smart grid / private LTE network.

name: FiberTower Spectrum Holdings
description: AT&T subsidiary used for mmWave spectrum bidding. Originally FiberTower Corporation which AT&T acquired out of bankruptcy.
notes: AT&T acquired FiberTower out of bankruptcy for $56M in 2013. FiberTower held 24/39 GHz spectrum. AT&T used entity to bid in Auction 103.

name: Frontier Wireless LLC
description: EchoStar/Dish entity that won E Block licenses in 700 MHz auction.
notes: Won 168 E block licenses in 700 MHz Auction 73 for $712M.

name: GeoVerse Inc.
description: Private LTE/5G network company focused on CBRS spectrum for enterprise and industrial IoT.
notes: Won $48M in CBRS spectrum (Auction 105). Focus on private LTE/5G networks for enterprise.

name: Google LLC
description: Bid in 700 MHz auction to trigger open access requirements on C Block. Did not win any licenses. Invested in Clearwire.
govt_contracts: Major federal cloud contracts (DoD JEDI follow-on, CIA)
defense_intel_connections: Significant government cloud contracts. Maven AI project (withdrawn after employee protests). Alphabet units work on defense/intelligence projects.
notes: Bid $4.6B on C Block in Auction 73 specifically to trigger open access rules (reserve was $4.6B). Did not win. Strategy was to force open network requirements on winner (Verizon). Invested in Clearwire.

name: Grain Management LLC
description: Private equity firm specializing in telecom infrastructure and spectrum. Founded by David Grain (former Sprint executive).
notes: David Grain, founder, former Sprint/US LEC executive. Active spectrum investor through Grain Spectrum LLC. Bid in multiple auctions.

name: Iridium Communications
description: Global satellite communications company. L-band spectrum for voice and data. Key opponent of Ligado plan.
govt_contracts: DoD EMSS contract (Enhanced Mobile Satellite Services), intelligence community contracts
defense_intel_connections: Original Iridium constellation partially funded by DoD. Current EMSS contract provides satellite phones to US military. L-band spectrum adjacent to Ligado -- strongly opposed Ligado terrestrial use. Used by intelligence community.
notes: Iridium NEXT constellation (66 satellites) launched 2017-2019. L-band spectrum at 1616-1626.5 MHz. Urged FCC to reverse Ligado L-band decision. DoD is significant customer.

name: King Street Wireless
description: Designated entity that won 700 MHz A Block licenses in rural Appalachian markets.
notes: Won $286M in 700 MHz A Block spectrum in Auction 73. Focus on rural/Appalachian markets.

name: L3Harris Technologies
description: Major defense contractor. Significant spectrum user for military communications, electronic warfare, and signal intelligence systems.
govt_contracts: Massive DoD contracts for tactical radios (AN/PRC-163, Falcon III), electronic warfare, ISR, night vision, space systems
defense_intel_connections: Acquired Azimuth Security and Linchpin Labs (zero-day exploit developers for Five Eyes/FBI, merged as "Trenchant"). $131.9M in lobbying 1999-2021 for ICE, DHS, intelligence. Builds radios used across DoD spectrum. Major GPS receiver manufacturer (opposed Ligado).
notes: L3 Technologies merged with Harris Corporation (2019). Key player in military spectrum policy. Builds tactical radios that depend on DoD spectrum allocations. Acquired zero-day companies for intelligence community.
source: SEC filings, lobbying disclosures, press reports

name: Ligado Networks
description: Formerly LightSquared. Holds controversial L-band spectrum (1525-1559/1626.5-1660.5 MHz) near GPS frequencies. Decade-long fight over terrestrial use.
defense_intel_connections: DoD, DoT, DHS all opposed FCC approval of Ligado L-band use due to GPS interference concerns. Pentagon conducted extensive testing showing interference. Multiple Congressional defense committee letters opposing. 2024: Ligado sued US government for "taking" of spectrum rights.
notes: Originally LightSquared, backed by Philip Falcone/Harbinger Capital. Went bankrupt in 2012 over GPS interference. FCC approved modified plan in 2020 despite Pentagon objections. DoD effectively blocked deployment. Ligado sued government in 2024.
source: FCC proceedings, Congressional testimony, press reports

name: Lockheed Martin
description: Largest defense contractor. GPS satellite manufacturer. Key voice in spectrum policy affecting military systems.
govt_contracts: F-35, GPS III satellites, Aegis Combat System, THAAD, Space Fence (S-band), numerous classified programs
defense_intel_connections: Builds GPS III satellites -- key stakeholder in Ligado L-band debate. Space Fence radar (S-band) for space surveillance. Extensive classified programs using spectrum. Contracts across DoD, NRO, CIA.
subsidiaries: ["Lockheed Martin Space", "Lockheed Martin Rotary & Mission Systems"]
notes: Does not hold commercial spectrum but builds systems dependent on specific bands. GPS III manufacturer -- strongly opposed Ligado L-band plan. Space Fence at Kwajalein uses S-band.

name: MetroPCS Communications
description: Prepaid wireless carrier that won AWS-1 spectrum. Merged with T-Mobile in 2013.
notes: Won $1.4B in AWS-1 spectrum (Auction 66). Merged with T-Mobile in 2013 reverse merger. Now Metro by T-Mobile brand.

name: Midcontinent Communications
description: Regional cable/broadband company in upper Midwest. Won C-band spectrum.
notes: Won $42M in C-band spectrum (Auction 107). Regional cable company serving Dakotas, Minnesota, Wisconsin.

name: NewLevel II LLC
description: Columbia Capital entity that won C-band spectrum.
notes: Columbia Capital investment entity. Won $422M in C-band spectrum (Auction 107).

name: NextWave Telecom
description: Won $4.2B in PCS C Block spectrum in 1996, then went bankrupt. Licenses tied up in courts for years. Landmark spectrum bankruptcy case.
notes: Allen Salmasi founded NextWave. Won $4.2B in Auction 5 (1996). Went bankrupt 1998. Supreme Court ruled FCC could not cancel licenses of bankrupt bidders (FCC v. NextWave, 2003). Eventually sold spectrum. Cautionary tale of overbidding.

name: Northrop Grumman
description: Defense contractor. Builds electronic warfare, radar, and signals intelligence systems dependent on spectrum access.
govt_contracts: B-21 Raider, AESA radar systems, BACN/E-11A, JSTARS, multiple classified SIGINT programs
defense_intel_connections: Builds electronic warfare and SIGINT systems. BACN (Battlefield Airborne Communications Node) operates in multiple spectrum bands. Acquired Orbital ATK for rocket/satellite capabilities.
subsidiaries: ["Northrop Grumman Mission Systems", "Northrop Grumman Space Systems"]
notes: Key player in military spectrum usage. Electronic warfare systems require access to wide spectrum swaths. Not a commercial spectrum holder.

name: Northstar Wireless LLC
description: Dish-controlled entity that bid in AWS-3 auction as designated entity. FCC determined it was a Dish front, denying $3.3B in bidding credits.
notes: Dish owned 85%. Won $7.6B in AWS-3 licenses. FCC found it was Dish-controlled, ineligible for DE discounts. Forfeited 197 of 702 licenses.

name: Omega Wireless LLC
description: Entity that won 600 MHz spectrum in the incentive auction.
notes: Won $95M in 600 MHz spectrum (Auction 1000).

name: PTI Pacifica Inc.
description: Pacific territories telecommunications provider. Won 2.5 GHz spectrum in Auction 108.
notes: Pacific territories carrier. Second largest bidder in Auction 108 at $18M.

name: ParkerB.com Wireless LLC
description: Dish subsidiary used for 600 MHz incentive auction bidding.
notes: Dish bidding entity in 600 MHz incentive auction. Won approximately $6.2B in spectrum.

name: PrimeCo Personal Communications
description: Joint venture of Bell Atlantic, NYNEX, AirTouch, and US West for PCS. Eventually dissolved, spectrum went to Verizon.
notes: Won $1.1B in PCS A/B Block spectrum (Auction 4). JV of Bell Atlantic, NYNEX, AirTouch, US West. Dissolved when partners merged into Verizon.

name: Qualcomm Incorporated
description: Wireless technology company. Holds some spectrum licenses. Major bid in 700 MHz auction. Developer of key wireless patents and chipsets.
defense_intel_connections: Qualcomm Government Technologies division. 5G research with DoD. Board members with defense backgrounds. CFIUS blocked Broadcom hostile takeover (2018) on national security grounds.
notes: Won $558M in 700 MHz auction (Auction 73). Sold most spectrum licenses. Primarily a technology/chipset company now. CFIUS blocked Broadcom acquisition citing 5G national security concerns.

name: Raytheon Technologies (RTX)
description: Major defense/aerospace contractor. Significant spectrum user for radar, electronic warfare, missile systems. Key player in spectrum sharing debates.
govt_contracts: Massive DoD contracts: Patriot missile, AN/TPY-2 radar, AESA radars, GPS III, numerous classified programs
defense_intel_connections: Among the largest defense contractors. Builds radar systems across multiple spectrum bands including S-band, C-band, X-band. Key opponent of commercial use of spectrum bands adjacent to military radar. Raytheon Intelligence & Space division.
subsidiaries: ["Raytheon Missiles & Defense", "Raytheon Intelligence & Space", "Collins Aerospace", "Pratt & Whitney"]
notes: Does not hold commercial spectrum licenses but is critical stakeholder in spectrum policy. Radar systems operate in bands adjacent to commercial auctions. Opposed C-band and 3.45 GHz commercial use near military radar.

name: Rivada Networks
description: Network technology company with defense/intelligence connections. Proposed dynamic spectrum sharing for DoD bands. Building LEO satellite constellation.
govt_contracts: US Secret Service TACCOM contract ($3B DHS umbrella), FirstNet bidder (Rivada Mercury consortium)
defense_intel_connections: Karl Rove as paid lobbyist. Newt Gingrich lobbying for DoD spectrum deal. Rivada Port Graham Solutions won DHS/Secret Service TACCOM contract. Lobbied for wholesale access to DoD 3.5 GHz spectrum. Rivada Mercury consortium with Harris Corp.
subsidiaries: ["Rivada Mercury LLC", "Rivada Port Graham Solutions", "Rivada Space Networks GmbH"]
notes: McLean, VA headquarters near intelligence community. Proposed DoD spectrum wholesale model opposed by carriers. Building 600-satellite LEO constellation ($2.4B Terran Orbital contract). Failed FirstNet bid.
source: Press reports, FCC filings, lobbying disclosures

name: SNR Wireless LicenseCo LLC
description: Second Dish-controlled entity in AWS-3 auction. Part of Dish strategy to obtain bidding credits through designated entities.
notes: Dish owned 85%. Won $5.7B in AWS-3 licenses. FCC denied DE status. Part of lawsuit alleging Dish used sham companies.

name: Salmon PCS LLC
description: Designated entity that won PCS spectrum in Auction 35.
notes: Won $2.4B in PCS C/F Block spectrum (Auction 35) as designated entity.

name: Sirius XM Holdings
description: Satellite radio company. Original DARS license winners from Auction 15 (1997).
notes: Sirius and XM separately won DARS spectrum in Auction 15 (1997) for combined $173M. Merged in 2008. Now controlled by Liberty Media. Spectrum in S-band (2320-2345 MHz).

name: SpaceX (Starlink)
description: Starlink satellite broadband constellation. Not an auction participant but holds significant spectrum authorizations via FCC grants.
govt_contracts: NASA Commercial Crew, DoD launch contracts, military Starlink Shield
defense_intel_connections: Starlink used by Ukraine military with DoD funding. Starlink Shield/Starshield for government/military use. Growing DoD satellite communications role.
notes: Has not participated in spectrum auctions. Ku/Ka-band spectrum via FCC license grants. V-band authorization pending. Growing conflict with terrestrial carriers over spectrum use.

name: SpectrumCo LLC
description: Joint venture of Comcast, Time Warner Cable, Bright House Networks, and Cox. Won AWS-1 spectrum, later sold to Verizon.
notes: Won $2.4B in AWS-1 spectrum (Auction 66). Sold all spectrum to Verizon for $3.6B in 2012. Included cross-marketing agreement between Verizon and cable companies.

name: Sprint Corporation
description: Former fourth-largest US carrier. Held massive 2.5 GHz spectrum portfolio via Clearwire. Merged with T-Mobile in 2020.
defense_intel_connections: Sprint Government Solutions (now T-Mobile for Government). Was major government mobility provider.
subsidiaries: ["Clearwire Corporation", "Sprint PCS", "Boost Mobile (now Dish)", "Virgin Mobile USA"]
notes: Merged with T-Mobile April 2020 ($26B deal). Brought 190+ MHz of 2.5 GHz spectrum. DoJ required divestiture of Boost Mobile to Dish as merger condition.

name: Starry Group Holdings
description: Fixed wireless broadband ISP using mmWave spectrum. Filed for bankruptcy in 2023.
notes: Won $85M in mmWave spectrum (Auction 103). Fixed wireless ISP in urban markets. Filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy 2023. Spectrum sold in bankruptcy proceedings.

name: Straight Path Communications
description: Spun off from IDT Corporation. Held valuable 28/39 GHz mmWave spectrum. Acquired by Verizon for $3.1B.
notes: Had over 700 mmWave licenses in 28/39 GHz bands. AT&T initially offered $1.6B but Verizon won bidding war at $3.1B (2018). Key 5G mmWave asset.

name: T-Mobile US
description: Third largest US carrier. Merged with Sprint (2020) gaining massive 2.5 GHz holdings. Dominant 600 MHz holder. Aggressive 5G deployment.
govt_contracts: Federal mobility contracts, some military base coverage
defense_intel_connections: Parent company Deutsche Telekom is partially owned by German government. Less direct US defense involvement than AT&T/Verizon.
subsidiaries: ["Sprint Corporation", "Metro by T-Mobile", "T-Mobile Spectrum LLC", "Clearwire Corporation"]
notes: Sprint merger (2020) brought massive 2.5 GHz spectrum. Dominated 600 MHz auction ($8B). Won 90% of 2.5 GHz Auction 108 licenses.

name: TeleGuam Holdings LLC
description: Guam telecommunications provider. Won 2.5 GHz spectrum in Auction 108.
defense_intel_connections: Guam is major US military installation hub (Andersen AFB, Naval Base Guam). Telecom on Guam intersects with military communications.
notes: Third largest bidder in Auction 108 at $17M. Strategic location for Pacific military operations.

name: Three Forty-Five Spectrum LLC
description: Investment entity that spent $1.3B in 3.45 GHz Auction 110. Fourth largest bidder.
notes: Spent $1.3B+ in Auction 110 for 3.45 GHz spectrum. Opaque ownership structure.

name: Triad 700 LLC
description: Entity that won 700 MHz A Block spectrum in Auction 73.
notes: Won $157M in 700 MHz A Block spectrum (Auction 73).

name: US Cellular Corporation
description: Regional wireless carrier. Active auction participant. Holdings in 700 MHz, CBRS, mmWave, C-band, and 3.45 GHz.
notes: T-Mobile agreed to acquire most of US Cellular spectrum and operations (2024). Active bidder in mmWave auctions.

name: Verizon Communications
description: Largest US wireless carrier by subscribers. Massive spectrum portfolio across 700 MHz, AWS, PCS, C-band, and mmWave. Single largest C-band bidder at $45.4B in Auction 107.
govt_contracts: Major federal government wireless contracts, FirstNet-related, DoD mobile contracts
defense_intel_connections: Verizon Business provides significant services to DoD, intelligence community. Former MCI/WorldCom government division. Operates fiber backbone used by government agencies.
subsidiaries: ["Cellco Partnership", "Verizon Wireless", "Straight Path Spectrum LLC", "TracFone Wireless"]
notes: Acquired Straight Path Communications (2018) for mmWave holdings. Acquired TracFone (2021). Did not bid in 600 MHz or 3.45 GHz auctions.

name: Vulcan Spectrum LLC
description: Paul Allen entity that bid in 700 MHz auction.
notes: Paul Allen (Microsoft co-founder) investment entity. Won $129M in 700 MHz spectrum. Allen also invested in Charter Communications.

name: Weminuche LLC
description: Dish subsidiary used for 3.45 GHz auction bidding. Spent $7.3B in Auction 110.
notes: Named after Weminuche Wilderness in Colorado. Won 1232 licenses in 3.45 GHz auction.

name: Windstream Holdings
description: Rural broadband provider. Active in mmWave and CBRS auctions. Went through bankruptcy in 2019.
govt_contracts: Rural broadband subsidies, CAF II, RDOF funding
notes: Filed bankruptcy 2019, emerged 2020. Won spectrum in Auction 101 ($17.4M), 102 ($48.2M), and 105 ($68M). Focus on fixed wireless/rural broadband.
